title: Alsine marina
taxonomy: Alsine marina is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae. This family is a part of the order Caryophyllales. Alsine marina is also commonly known as sea campion. It is a species of flowering plant native to coastal areas of Europe and North Africa. Alsine marina is a type of succulent that grows in the intertidal zone.
uses: Alsine marina, also known as sea campion, is a flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of cell damage. In addition, sea campion has been used to treat various ailments, including arthritis, digestive issues, and wounds. However, it's essential to note that the efficacy and safety of Alsine marina for medicinal purposes have not been extensively scientifically proven. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and potential interactions with other medications.

intro: Alsine marina, also known as sea campion, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been found to possess potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Alsine marina include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from sea campion include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, often used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
